message:
feat(fleet): add FleetProgress struct for parallel deployment tracking

Part of bd-11hn: Add nested progress for parallel fleet operations

Adds:
- rch/src/fleet/progress.rs with FleetProgress struct
- DeployPhase enum for tracking deployment stages
- Per-worker progress bars using MultiProgressManager
- Support for phase transitions: Queued -> Connecting -> Uploading -> Installing -> Verifying -> Complete
- Tests for all progress tracking functionality

Next step: Integrate FleetProgress into FleetExecutor::deploy_batch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

## MANDATORY Logging
This test MUST use TestLogger for all phases:


### Test Filtering
4. Filter by name:
   - Command: `cargo test test_name_pattern`

   - Log: filter pattern, tests matched

5. Run ignored tests:
   - Command: `cargo test -- --ignored`

   - Log: ignored tests count

6. Show output:

   - Log: output capture mode

### Test Targets
7. Lib tests only:
   - Command: `cargo test --lib`
   - Expected: only library tests
   - Log: target filter

   - Command: `cargo test --doc`

9. Test thread control:

    - Command: `cargo test --workspace`

- 0 = all tests passed (log: success)
- 1 = compilation/build error (log: build_error)

-{"id":"bd-11hn","title":"Add nested progress for parallel fleet operations","description":"# Add Nested Progress for Fleet Operations

## Problem
`rch fleet deploy` deploys to multiple workers simultaneously but shows no per-worker progress. Users see nothing until all workers complete.

## Current Behavior
```bash
$ rch fleet deploy
# ... nothing for 2-5 minutes ...
Deployed to 5 workers.
```

## Proposed Behavior
```
Deploying rch-wkr to fleet (2/5 complete)

  css   ████████████████████ 100% ✓ (0.1.2 installed)
  csd   ████████████████████ 100% ✓ (0.1.2 installed)
  cse   ████████████░░░░░░░░  60% uploading binary...
  csf   ████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  20% connecting...
  csg   ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░   0% queued

Elapsed: 45s | ETA: ~30s
```

## Implementation

### Nested MultiProgress
```rust
struct FleetProgress {
    multi: MultiProgress,
    overall: ProgressBar,
    workers: HashMap<WorkerId, ProgressBar>,
}

impl FleetProgress {
    fn new(workers: &[WorkerConfig]) -> Self {
        let multi = MultiProgress::new();
        
        // Overall progress bar at top
        let overall = multi.add(ProgressBar::new(workers.len() as u64));
        overall.set_style(ProgressStyle::default_bar()
            .template("Deploying ({pos}/{len})\n")
            .unwrap());
        
        // Per-worker bars
        let worker_bars = workers.iter().map(|w| {
            let pb = multi.add(ProgressBar::new(100));
            pb.set_prefix(format\!("  {:6}", w.id));
            (w.id.clone(), pb)
        }).collect();
        
        Self { multi, overall, workers: worker_bars }
    }
    
    fn worker_progress(&self, id: &WorkerId, pct: u64, msg: &str) {
        if let Some(pb) = self.workers.get(id) {
            pb.set_position(pct);
            pb.set_message(msg.to_string());
        }
    }
    
    fn worker_complete(&self, id: &WorkerId, success: bool) {
        if let Some(pb) = self.workers.get(id) {
            if success {
                pb.finish_with_message("✓");
            } else {
                pb.abandon_with_message("✗");
            }
        }
        self.overall.inc(1);
    }
}
```

## Fleet Commands Needing This
- `rch fleet deploy` - Deploy binaries
- `rch fleet drain --all` - Drain all workers
- `rch fleet upgrade` - Upgrade all workers
- `rch workers probe --all` - Probe all workers
- `rch workers benchmark` - Benchmark all workers

- rch/src/commands.rs (use FleetProgress in handlers)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:09:35.154998396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:09:45.902202062Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11hn","depends_on_id":"bd-2tbk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:09:45.902159502Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-11wb","title":"Idea: Configurable SSH keepalive + ControlPersist","description":"## Background
Long-running builds or idle periods can drop SSH connections. Exposing keepalive/ControlPersist settings improves stability across varied networks.

## Goals
- Add config options for SSH keepalive intervals and ControlPersist duration.
- Maintain current defaults for users who do nothing.

## Design / Approach
- Extend SshOptions to include keepalive settings.
- Pass options into openssh SessionBuilder or SSH command flags (for rsync).
- Expose in config and docs.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add config fields + validation.
- Wire into SshClient and rsync `-e` command.
- Add doc examples.

## Tests
- Unit: config parsing.
- Integration: mock ensures SSH command includes keepalive flags.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can tune SSH keepalive without code changes.
- Default behavior unchanged.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-11wb.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Invalid keepalive values -> use defaults and warn.
- Apply settings consistently for SSH exec and rsync `-e`.
- If platform ignores settings, continue without failure.

## E2E Outline
- Configure keepalive -> rsync command includes flags.
- Mock SSH client receives keepalive options.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch-common/src/ssh.rs: keepalive/controlpersist mapping to SSH options.
- rch/src/transfer.rs: rsync -e includes keepalive flags.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-11wb.sh: configure keepalive, verify rsync args and SSH session settings.

 {"id":"bd-12h6","title":"E2E: Daemon Stability Test with rich_rust","description":"## Test: rchd Daemon Stability

Verify that rchd daemon functions correctly with rich_rust integration. The daemon is a long-running service that must remain stable.

### Test Scenarios

#### 1. Startup and Shutdown
```bash
# Clean startup
rchd start &
sleep 2
rchd stop

# Verify clean shutdown
ps aux | grep rchd | grep -v grep && fail "Daemon still running"
```

#### 2. Rich Output Doesn't Block
```bash
# Daemon should not block on rich output if stderr is not a TTY
rchd start > /dev/null 2>&1 &
DAEMON_PID=$!

sleep 2
kill -0 $DAEMON_PID || fail "Daemon died unexpectedly"

# Perform operations
rch status || true
rch workers list || true

# Daemon should still be healthy
kill -0 $DAEMON_PID || fail "Daemon crashed during operations"

rchd stop
```

#### 3. High Volume Job Processing
```bash
# Process 100 jobs rapidly
for i in $(seq 1 100); do
    echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo test"}}' | rch hook --stdin &
done
wait

# Daemon should not OOM or crash
rch status || fail "Daemon unhealthy after load test"
```

#### 4. Memory Stability (No Leaks)
```bash
# Monitor memory over time
INITIAL_MEM=$(ps -o rss= -p $DAEMON_PID)

# Process 1000 jobs
for i in $(seq 1 1000); do
    echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo test"}}' | rch hook --stdin >/dev/null 2>&1
done

FINAL_MEM=$(ps -o rss= -p $DAEMON_PID)
GROWTH=$((FINAL_MEM - INITIAL_MEM))

# Allow some growth but not unbounded
if (( GROWTH > 50000 )); then  # 50MB
    fail "Memory growth too high: ${GROWTH}KB"
fi
```

### Implementation

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/test_daemon_stability.sh
set -euo pipefail

source scripts/test_lib.sh
init_test_log "daemon_stability"

RCHD="./target/release/rchd"
RCH="./target/release/rch"

cleanup() {
    "$RCH" daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    pkill -f rchd 2>/dev/null || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log_json "setup" "Building binaries"
cargo build -p rch -p rchd --features rich-ui --release

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 1: Startup/Shutdown Cycle
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Startup/shutdown cycle"

for i in 1 2 3; do
    "$RCH" daemon start >/dev/null 2>&1
    sleep 1
    
    if ! "$RCH" status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        fail "Daemon not responding on cycle $i"
    fi
    
    "$RCH" daemon stop >/dev/null 2>&1
    sleep 1
done

log_json "verify" "Startup/shutdown cycles passed" '{"cycles":3}'

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 2: Background Operation (no TTY)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Background operation without TTY"

# Start daemon with all output redirected
"$RCH" daemon start > /tmp/daemon_stdout.txt 2> /tmp/daemon_stderr.txt &
sleep 2

if ! "$RCH" status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "Daemon failed in background mode"
fi

# Rich output should go to stderr, not block startup
if [[ -s /tmp/daemon_stderr.txt ]]; then
    log_json "info" "Daemon stderr output" "{\"bytes\":$(wc -c < /tmp/daemon_stderr.txt)}"
fi

"$RCH" daemon stop

log_json "verify" "Background operation passed" '{"passed":true}'

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 3: Load Test
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Load test (100 concurrent hook invocations)"

"$RCH" daemon start
sleep 2

START=$(date +%s%N)
for i in $(seq 1 100); do
    echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo test"}}' | "$RCH" hook --stdin >/dev/null 2>&1 &
done
wait
END=$(date +%s%N)

DURATION_MS=$(( (END - START) / 1000000 ))
log_json "timing" "Load test duration" "{\"jobs\":100,\"duration_ms\":$DURATION_MS}"

if ! "$RCH" status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "Daemon unhealthy after load test"
fi

"$RCH" daemon stop

log_json "verify" "Load test passed" "{\"jobs\":100,\"duration_ms\":$DURATION_MS}"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SUMMARY
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "summary" "All daemon stability tests passed"
echo ""
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo "ALL DAEMON STABILITY TESTS PASSED"
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
```

 {"id":"bd-12hi","title":"True E2E Cargo Compilation Tests (build/test/check/clippy)","description":"# Feature: True E2E Cargo Compilation Tests

## Purpose

Validate that all Cargo-based compilation kinds work correctly when executed on real remote workers. This is the **most critical** test category because Rust/Cargo is the primary use case for RCH.

## MANDATORY Logging Requirements

All tests in this feature MUST use the structured JSON logging infrastructure from bd-3saj:

### Log Events Per Test

```json
// Test start
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"setup","msg":"Starting Cargo build test","data":{"fixture":"simple_binary","worker":"css"}}

// Sync phase
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"sync","msg":"Project synced","data":{"files":42,"bytes":123456,"duration_ms":234}}

// Local execution
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"execute_local","msg":"Running local cargo build","data":{"cmd":"cargo build","exit_code":0,"duration_ms":5432}}

// Remote execution
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"execute_remote","msg":"Running remote cargo build","data":{"cmd":"cargo build","exit_code":0,"worker":"css","duration_ms":3210}}

// Comparison
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"validate","msg":"Outputs match","data":{"stdout_match":true,"stderr_match":true,"exit_code_match":true}}

// Artifact check
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"validate","msg":"Artifact verified","data":{"path":"target/debug/mybin","size":1234567,"exists":true}}

// Test end
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"teardown","msg":"Test completed","data":{"result":"pass","total_duration_ms":9876}}
```

### Failure Logging

On any assertion failure, log detailed diff information:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"ERROR","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"validate","msg":"Output mismatch","data":{"field":"stdout","local_lines":42,"remote_lines":43,"diff":"<unified diff>"}}
```

## CompilationKind Coverage

The following `CompilationKind` variants must have true e2e tests:

| Kind | Command Example | Priority | Notes |
|------|-----------------|----------|-------|
| CargoBuild | `cargo build --release` | P0 | Core functionality |
| CargoTest | `cargo test` | P0 | Tests must report correctly |
| CargoCheck | `cargo check` | P1 | Fast validation |
| CargoClippy | `cargo clippy` | P1 | Lints must match local |
| CargoBench | `cargo bench` | P2 | Benchmark output |
| CargoNextest | `cargo nextest run` | P2 | Alternative test runner |
| CargoDoc | `cargo doc` | P3 | Documentation generation |

## Test Scenarios Per Kind

### CargoBuild Tests
1. **Simple build** - Single binary crate
2. **Workspace build** - Multi-crate workspace
3. **Release build** - `--release` flag honored
4. **Feature flags** - `--features` passed correctly
5. **Target directory** - Artifacts in correct location
6. **Incremental rebuild** - Only changed files recompiled

### CargoTest Tests
1. **All tests pass** - Exit code 0, output matches
2. **Some tests fail** - Exit code 101, failure output captured
3. **Filtered tests** - `cargo test my_test` runs subset
4. **Test threads** - `--test-threads=1` honored
5. **Nocapture** - `-- --nocapture` shows println output
6. **Doc tests** - `--doc` flag runs doc tests

### CargoCheck Tests
1. **Clean check** - No warnings
2. **With warnings** - Warnings captured in output
3. **With errors** - Compilation errors reported correctly

### CargoClippy Tests
1. **No warnings** - Clean clippy run
2. **With lints** - Lint warnings match local output
3. **Deny warnings** - `-D warnings` causes failure correctly

## Test Project Templates

Create minimal test projects in `tests/true_e2e/fixtures/`:

```
fixtures/
├── simple_binary/      # Single main.rs binary
├── simple_library/     # Single lib.rs library  
├── with_tests/         # Has #[test] functions
├── with_failures/      # Has tests that fail
├── workspace/          # Multi-crate workspace
└── with_build_rs/      # Custom build script
```

## Output Validation

For each test, validate AND LOG:
1. **Exit code** matches local execution (log both values)
2. **Compiled artifacts** exist and are valid (log path, size, hash)
3. **Stdout/stderr** match with path normalization (log diff on mismatch)
4. **Timing** is within reasonable bounds (log local vs remote ms)

## Special Considerations

### Test Isolation
Each test should use a unique temporary copy of fixtures to prevent state pollution between tests. Log fixture copy path.

### Toolchain Compatibility
Tests should detect if worker has compatible Rust version and skip if not. Log skip reason as structured JSON.

### Workspace Member Handling
Ensure `-p package_name` works correctly for workspace builds.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CargoBuild: simple, workspace, release, features tests pass
- [ ] CargoTest: pass, fail, filtered, nocapture tests pass
- [ ] CargoCheck: clean, warnings, errors tests pass
- [ ] CargoClippy: clean, with-lints tests pass

 {"id":"bd-23n3","title":"True E2E Error Handling & Recovery Tests","description":"# Feature: True E2E Error Handling & Recovery Tests

## Purpose

Test that rch correctly handles and recovers from various error conditions during remote compilation, ensuring the fail-open philosophy is upheld.

## MANDATORY Logging Requirements

Error handling tests MUST use structured JSON logging to capture:

### Error-Specific Log Events

```json
// Error scenario setup
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_worker_unreachable","phase":"setup","msg":"Simulating error condition","data":{"error_type":"network","simulation":"iptables DROP","target":"css:22"}}

// Error detection
{"ts":"...","level":"WARN","test":"test_worker_unreachable","phase":"connect","msg":"Connection failed (expected)","data":{"worker":"css","error":"Connection refused","code":"ECONNREFUSED","timeout_ms":5000}}

// Fallback behavior
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_worker_unreachable","phase":"fallback","msg":"Falling back to local","data":{"reason":"all_workers_unavailable","workers_tried":["css"],"local_execution":true}}

// Circuit breaker state
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_circuit_breaker","phase":"circuit","msg":"Circuit breaker state change","data":{"worker":"css","previous_state":"closed","new_state":"open","failure_count":5,"cooldown_ms":30000}}

// Recovery verification
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_circuit_breaker","phase":"recovery","msg":"Circuit breaker recovered","data":{"worker":"css","state":"half_open","probe_success":true}}

// Exit code propagation
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_propagation","phase":"verify","msg":"Exit code check","data":{"expected":101,"actual":101,"match":true,"source":"remote"}}
```

### Error Metrics to Log

1. **Error type**: network, worker, protocol, timeout
2. **Error details**: code, message, worker affected
3. **Recovery actions**: fallback, retry, skip
4. **Timing**: time to detect, time to recover
5. **Circuit breaker**: state transitions, failure counts

## Why This Matters

- rch follows a fail-open philosophy: errors should allow local execution, not block
- Network failures, SSH timeouts, worker crashes are common in distributed systems
- Incorrect error handling could leave agents stuck or produce silent failures
- Exit codes from remote commands must be correctly propagated

## Error Scenarios to Test

### Network-Level Errors

1. **Worker unreachable** (SSH connection refused)
   - Log: connection attempt, error, fallback decision
   
2. **Network timeout during transfer**
   - Log: transfer start, timeout trigger, partial state
   
3. **Connection drop mid-compilation**
   - Log: compilation start, disconnect, recovery attempt
   
4. **DNS resolution failure**
   - Log: hostname, lookup failure, fallback
   
5. **SSH key authentication failure**
   - Log: key type tried, rejection reason (no actual key content!)

### Worker-Level Errors

1. **Worker out of disk space**
   - Log: write attempt, ENOSPC error, cleanup actions
   
2. **Worker out of memory**
   - Log: compilation start, OOM, process termination
   
3. **Worker has no free slots**
   - Log: slot request, capacity, queue state
   
4. **Worker process crash**
   - Log: process start, crash signal, core dump (if any)
   
5. **Worker toolchain missing**
   - Log: toolchain check, missing components, skip reason

### Protocol-Level Errors

1. **Daemon socket not available**
   - Log: socket path, connection error, local fallback
   
2. **Malformed daemon response**
   - Log: request sent, response received (sanitized), parse error
   
3. **Protocol version mismatch**
   - Log: local version, daemon version, compatibility check
   
4. **Request timeout**
   - Log: request start, timeout value, cancellation

### Recovery Scenarios

1. **Automatic retry on transient failure**
   - Log: failure, retry count, eventual success/failure
   
2. **Fallback to next available worker**
   - Log: first worker failure, second worker selection, success
   
3. **Graceful fallback to local execution**
   - Log: remote failures, local execution, result
   
4. **Circuit breaker tripping and recovery**
   - Log: all state transitions, probe attempts, recovery

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All error scenarios properly detected and logged
- [ ] Fail-open behavior verified (local fallback works)
- [ ] Exit codes correctly propagated
- [ ] No silent failures or stuck states
- [ ] Circuit breaker correctly trips and recovers
- [ ] ALL tests emit structured JSON logs with error details
- [ ] Recovery actions logged with timing

## Technical Notes

- May need to simulate errors with iptables/tc for network tests
- Worker errors can be simulated with ulimit
- Daemon errors can be tested by killing/restarting daemon
- Log simulation setup/teardown for reproducibility

## Dependencies

- Requires bd-3saj (test infrastructure)
- Requires bd-255k (SSH tests for connection handling)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"ScarletOwl","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:20:46.778707543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:33:35.472916459Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:33:35.472848382Z","close_reason":"Error handling E2E tests complete: circuit breaker (open/half-open/recovery), probe failures/timeouts, remote command failures/timeouts, worker selection.  {"id":"bd-243w","title":"Idea: Adaptive zstd compression level","description":"## Background
A fixed compression level is suboptimal: small diffs benefit from low compression, while large syncs benefit from higher compression. Adaptive selection can improve overall latency.

## Goals
- Dynamically select zstd compression level based on estimated transfer size.
- Preserve existing default behavior if estimation is unavailable.

## Design / Approach
- Use transfer estimator or rsync stats to classify payload size.
- Map size buckets to compression levels (e.g., <10MB -> level 1, 10-200MB -> level 3, >200MB -> level 7).
- Allow user override to disable adaptive mode.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add config toggle: transfer.adaptive_compression = true/false.
- Implement mapping and fallbacks.
- Emit debug logs with chosen level.

## Tests
- Unit: bucket mapping for sizes.
- Integration: rsync command uses expected `--compress-level`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Compression level changes based on payload size when enabled.
- Manual config overrides still work.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-243w.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Adaptive mode off -> use configured compression unchanged.
- Estimator failure -> fallback to default level.
- Cap compression level to avoid CPU spikes.

## E2E Outline
- Small payload -> low compression level in rsync args.
- Large payload -> higher level.
- Disable adaptive -> level stays fixed.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/transfer.rs: size bucket -> compression level mapping.
- config validation for adaptive toggle.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-243w.sh: small/large payload -> expected rsync args.

 {"id":"bd-255k","title":"True E2E SSH Infrastructure Tests","description":"# Feature: True E2E SSH Infrastructure Tests

## Purpose

Validate that RCHs SSH infrastructure works correctly with real SSH connections. The mock tests bypass actual SSH, so we need tests that verify:

1. Real SSH key authentication works
2. SSH connection pooling behaves correctly
3. SSH agent forwarding functions properly
4. Connection timeouts and reconnection work
5. Various key types (Ed25519, RSA, ECDSA) are supported

## MANDATORY Logging Requirements

All SSH tests MUST use structured JSON logging:

### SSH-Specific Log Events

```json
// Connection attempt
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_ssh_ed25519_auth","phase":"connect","msg":"SSH connection attempt","data":{"worker":"css","key_type":"ed25519","port":22}}

// Authentication result
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_ssh_ed25519_auth","phase":"auth","msg":"SSH authentication succeeded","data":{"worker":"css","key_type":"ed25519","auth_duration_ms":123}}

// Pool stats
{"ts":"...","level":"DEBUG","test":"test_connection_reuse","phase":"pool","msg":"Connection pool stats","data":{"active":1,"idle":2,"total_created":3,"reused":5}}

// Command execution over SSH
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_ssh_command","phase":"execute","msg":"SSH command executed","data":{"cmd":"uname -a","exit_code":0,"stdout_bytes":42,"duration_ms":56}}

// Connection error (logged but may be expected)
{"ts":"...","level":"WARN","test":"test_wrong_key_rejection","phase":"auth","msg":"SSH authentication failed (expected)","data":{"worker":"css","key_type":"rsa","error":"Permission denied"}}
```

### Sensitive Data Handling

- NEVER log actual key material or passphrases
- Log key file paths, key types, and fingerprints only
- Mask any authentication tokens in logs

## Why SSH Tests Matter

RCH depends heavily on SSH for:
- **Control channel**: Command execution via SSH
- **Data transfer**: rsync over SSH for project sync
- **Connection pooling**: Reuse connections for performance
- **Authentication**: Key-based auth (no passwords)

If any of these break, the entire system fails silently or with cryptic errors.

## Test Scenarios

### Authentication Tests

1. **Ed25519 key authentication**
   - Most common modern key type
   - Verify connection succeeds with Ed25519 key
   - Log: key type, auth duration, success/fail
   
2. **RSA key authentication**
   - Legacy but still widely used
   - Verify RSA-4096 works
   - Log: key type, key size (inferred), auth duration
   
3. **ECDSA key authentication**
   - Alternative elliptic curve
   - Verify ECDSA keys work
   - Log: curve type if detectable
   
4. **SSH agent authentication**
   - Key loaded in ssh-agent
   - No explicit identity_file in config
   - Verify `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` is honored
   - Log: agent socket path (masked), loaded key count
   
5. **Wrong key rejection**
   - Provide invalid key
   - Verify graceful failure with clear error
   - Log: expected failure with error message

### Connection Pool Tests

1. **Connection reuse**
   - Multiple commands to same worker
   - Verify ControlMaster socket is reused
   - Measure latency improvement
   - Log: connection reuse count, latency savings
   
2. **Pool cleanup**
   - Connections closed after idle timeout
   - No leaked sockets
   - Log: pool stats before/after cleanup
   
3. **Concurrent connections**
   - Multiple simultaneous commands
   - Verify multiplexing works
   - Log: concurrent connection count, all results

### Resilience Tests

1. **Connection timeout**
   - Worker becomes unreachable mid-connection
   - Verify timeout triggers and fails gracefully
   - Log: timeout value, actual wait time, error
   
2. **Reconnection after disconnect**
   - Connection drops between commands
   - Verify automatic reconnection works
   - Log: disconnect reason, reconnect attempts, success
   
3. **Network blip recovery**
   - Brief network interruption
   - Verify connection recovers
   - Log: blip duration, recovery time

### Configuration Tests

1. **Custom SSH port**
   - Worker on non-standard port
   - Verify port configuration honored
   - Log: configured port, actual connection port
   
2. **ProxyJump/bastion host**
   - Worker behind bastion
   - Verify jump host configuration works
   - Log: hop count, intermediate hosts (names only)

## Test Infrastructure

### SSH Test Utilities

```rust
pub struct SSHTestContext {
    worker: WorkerInfo,
    key_path: PathBuf,
    key_type: KeyType,
    logger: TestLogger,  // MANDATORY
}

impl SSHTestContext {
    /// Execute simple command to verify SSH works (logs attempt + result)
    pub async fn verify_connection(&mut self) -> Result<()>;
    
    /// Get connection pool stats (logs stats)
    pub async fn pool_stats(&mut self) -> PoolStats;
    
    /// Forcibly close all connections (logs closure)
    pub async fn close_all_connections(&mut self);
}
```

### Key Type Detection

Tests should detect available key types on the system and test whichever are available, skipping unavailable types. Log which key types were found and which were skipped.

## Considerations

### Security
- Test keys should be test-only, not production keys
- Tests should not expose key material in logs
- Failed auth attempts should not lock accounts
- Log key fingerprints, never key contents

### CI Compatibility
- These tests require real SSH infrastructure
- Skip in CI environments without workers
- Document manual test execution
- Log skip reasons as structured JSON

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Ed25519 authentication test passes
- [ ] RSA authentication test passes (if RSA key available)
- [ ] SSH agent authentication test passes
- [ ] Connection pooling test verifies socket reuse
- [ ] Reconnection test verifies recovery
- [ ] All tests skip gracefully without real workers
- [ ] ALL tests emit structured JSON logs per phase
- [ ] No sensitive key material appears in logs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:11:18.132330350Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:44:23.514818840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:44:23.514780528Z","close_reason":"SSH Infrastructure Tests complete: ssh_tests.rs (12 tests) covers Ed25519/RSA/ECDSA auth, SSH agent, wrong key rejection, connection timeout, pool reuse/no leaks, health check, multi-worker, and reconnection. ssh_command_tests.rs (13 tests) covers echo, exit codes, stderr, working dir, env vars, long/binary output, timing, errors, pipes, and subshells.  {"id":"bd-27l6","title":"Test: Terminal Color & ANSI Code Preservation","description":"## Purpose
Test that terminal colors and ANSI formatting are preserved when output is streamed from remote workers.

## Test Cases

### ANSI Color Preservation
1. CARGO_TERM_COLOR=always:
   - Set env var, run `cargo build`
   - Verify: ANSI escape codes in output
   - Verify: colors visible in terminal

2. TERM=xterm-256color:
   - Run command with TERM set
   - Verify: worker respects TERM
   - Verify: colors match local execution

3. NO_COLOR=1:
   - Set NO_COLOR, run command
   - Verify: no ANSI codes in output

### Color Scenarios
4. cargo test colors:
   - Green for passing tests
   - Red for failing tests
   - Verify: colors preserved

5. cargo clippy colors:
   - Yellow for warnings
   - Red for errors
   - Verify: colors match local

6. Compiler error colors:
   - Error highlighting
   - Line number colors
   - Verify: all preserved

### Edge Cases
7. Non-color ANSI codes:
   - Bold, underline, blink
   - Verify: preserved in output

8. Wide characters:
   - Unicode in output
   - Verify: correctly transmitted

9. Long colored output:
   - Large output with many colors
   - Verify: no corruption

## Verification Method
- Capture raw bytes from local and remote
- Compare byte-for-byte for ANSI sequences
- Visual inspection in real terminal

## Acceptance Criteria
- ANSI escape codes byte-for-byte identical

-{"id":"bd-35dw","title":"Simplify rch init wizard flags to reduce confusion","description":"# Simplify Init Wizard Flags

## Problem
`rch init` has potentially conflicting flags:
- --wizard (interactive mode)
- --non-interactive (automated mode)  
- --yes (accept defaults)
- --skip-test (skip test compilation)

The combination `--wizard --non-interactive` is contradictory.

## Current Flags (main.rs ~196)
```rust
Init {
    #[arg(long, short = 'y')]
    yes: bool,
    #[arg(long)]
    skip_test: bool,
}
```

## Proposed Simplification

### Remove --wizard flag
The init command IS the wizard. No need for a separate flag.

### Clarify --yes behavior
`--yes` means 'accept all defaults without prompting' (non-interactive).

### Final Flag Set
```rust
Init {
    /// Accept all defaults without prompting
    #[arg(long, short = 'y')]
    yes: bool,
    
    /// Skip the test compilation step
    #[arg(long)]
    skip_test: bool,
    
    /// Skip worker discovery, use config file only
    #[arg(long)]
    no_discover: bool,
}
```

## Documentation Update
```
rch init              # Interactive wizard
rch init --yes        # Non-interactive with defaults
rch init --skip-test  # Interactive, skip final test
rch init -y --skip-test  # Fully automated
```

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/main.rs (Init command flags)
- rch/src/commands.rs (init handler)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:14:02.930643530Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:14:02.930643530Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-36x8","title":"CRITICAL: E2E Hook Context Non-Interference Test","description":"## THE MOST CRITICAL TEST FOR THIS ENTIRE INTEGRATION

This test verifies that AI coding agents (the PRIMARY users of RCH) are completely unaffected by rich_rust integration. ANY regression here is a BLOCKER.

### What This Tests

When rch is invoked via Claude Code PreToolUse hook:
1. stdout JSON responses are BYTE-FOR-BYTE identical to pre-integration
2. NO rich output appears on stdout (zero bytes of ANSI codes)
3. Exit codes are EXACTLY preserved (0, 1, 101, 128+N)
4. Timing is not degraded (hook classification still <1ms)
5. stderr can have rich output but ONLY if not captured by agent

### Test Implementation

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/test_hook_non_interference.sh
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
TEST_LOG="$PROJECT_ROOT/target/test_hook_interference.log"

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; }
pass() { log "✓ PASS: $*"; }
fail() { log "✗ FAIL: $*"; exit 1; }

# Build rch with rich-ui enabled
log "Building rch with rich-ui feature..."
cargo build -p rch --features rich-ui --release

RCH="$PROJECT_ROOT/target/release/rch"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 1: Hook JSON Response Integrity
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 1: Hook JSON Response Integrity"

# Simulate hook input (Tier-0 passthrough: 'echo')
HOOK_INPUT='{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo hello"}}'

# Capture stdout and stderr separately
STDOUT_FILE="$(mktemp)"
STDERR_FILE="$(mktemp)"
trap "rm -f $STDOUT_FILE $STDERR_FILE" EXIT

echo "$HOOK_INPUT" | "$RCH" hook --stdin > "$STDOUT_FILE" 2> "$STDERR_FILE"

# Verify stdout is valid JSON
if ! jq -e . "$STDOUT_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "stdout is not valid JSON"
fi

# Verify stdout contains NO ANSI escape codes
if grep -qP '\x1b\[' "$STDOUT_FILE"; then
    fail "stdout contains ANSI escape codes!"
fi

# Verify stdout has expected structure
if ! jq -e '.result' "$STDOUT_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "stdout missing .result field"
fi

pass "Hook JSON response is valid and clean"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 2: Hook Exit Codes Preserved  
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 2: Hook Exit Codes Preserved"

# Test allow response (exit 0)
ALLOW_INPUT='{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo hello"}}'
echo "$ALLOW_INPUT" | "$RCH" hook --stdin >/dev/null 2>&1
ALLOW_EXIT=$?

if [[ $ALLOW_EXIT -ne 0 ]]; then
    fail "Allow response should exit 0, got $ALLOW_EXIT"
fi
pass "Allow response exits 0"

# Test intercept response (exit 101 is handled by daemon, so test structure)
# ... additional exit code tests ...

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 3: Hook Classification Timing (<1ms for Tier-0)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 3: Hook Classification Timing"

# Run 100 hook invocations and measure average
ITERATIONS=100
TIMING_LOG="$(mktemp)"

for i in $(seq 1 $ITERATIONS); do
    START=$(date +%s%N)
    echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo test"}}' | "$RCH" hook --stdin >/dev/null 2>&1
    END=$(date +%s%N)
    echo $((END - START)) >> "$TIMING_LOG"
done

AVG_NS=$(awk '{ sum += $1 } END { print int(sum/NR) }' "$TIMING_LOG")
AVG_MS=$(echo "scale=2; $AVG_NS / 1000000" | bc)

log "Average hook time: ${AVG_MS}ms over $ITERATIONS iterations"

# Threshold: 5ms (generous for CI variance, real target is <1ms)
if (( AVG_NS > 5000000 )); then
    fail "Hook classification too slow: ${AVG_MS}ms (threshold: 5ms)"
fi
pass "Hook classification timing acceptable: ${AVG_MS}ms"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 4: Comparison with rich-ui DISABLED
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 4: Comparison with rich-ui disabled"

# Build without rich-ui
cargo build -p rch --no-default-features --release --target-dir target/no-rich

NO_RICH_RCH="$PROJECT_ROOT/target/no-rich/release/rch"

# Compare outputs byte-for-byte
echo "$HOOK_INPUT" | "$RCH" hook --stdin > /tmp/with_rich.json 2>/dev/null
echo "$HOOK_INPUT" | "$NO_RICH_RCH" hook --stdin > /tmp/no_rich.json 2>/dev/null

if ! diff /tmp/with_rich.json /tmp/no_rich.json; then
    fail "stdout differs between rich-ui enabled/disabled builds!"
fi
pass "stdout identical with/without rich-ui feature"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SUMMARY
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log ""
log "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
log "ALL HOOK NON-INTERFERENCE TESTS PASSED"
log "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
```

6. [ ] Test runs in CI on every PR

 {"id":"bd-377q","title":"Idea: Artifact integrity verification (blake3)","description":"## Background
RCH relies on rsync for artifact retrieval; silent corruption is unlikely but high impact. Optional integrity checks provide confidence for critical builds.

## Goals
- Optionally verify key artifacts via blake3 hashes post-transfer.
- Keep disabled by default to avoid extra overhead.

## Design / Approach
- Add config: transfer.verify_artifacts = true/false.
- For known artifact patterns, compute blake3 on worker and locally; compare.
- On mismatch, surface actionable error and fall back to local execution on next run.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add verification routine to transfer pipeline.
- Determine which artifacts to verify (binaries, test reports).
- Extend mock transport to simulate mismatch.

## Tests
- Unit: hash comparison and failure handling.
- Integration: mock mismatch triggers error message.
- E2E: verification disabled by default.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Integrity verification is optional and reliable.
- Clear error messages on mismatch.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-377q.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Skip verification if artifact missing or exceeds size cap.
- Hash on worker before download to avoid duplicate I/O.
- Mismatch error includes both hashes for debugging.

## E2E Outline
- Mock mismatch -> clear error with hashes.
- Verification disabled -> no extra output.
- Successful verification logged with duration.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/transfer.rs: hash compute + compare; size cap skip.
- error path includes both hashes and artifact name.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-377q.sh: mock mismatch -> clear error with hashes.

 {"id":"bd-37hc","title":"Idea: Worker selection audit log","description":"## Background
When selection decisions surprise users, they need a clear explanation of why a worker was chosen or skipped. An audit log makes selection debuggable.

## Goals
- Record per-selection decision inputs and scored outputs.
- Expose via `rch status --workers --verbose` or new `rch selection explain`.
- Keep overhead low and data bounded.

## Design / Approach
- Add structured selection events (scores, weights, reasons).
- Keep ring buffer in daemon with size cap.
- Optionally expose JSON endpoint for tooling.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Extend selection logic to emit decision records.
- Add API + CLI display.
- Add config to cap history size.

## Tests
- Unit: event generation format.
- Integration: CLI shows last decision with reasons.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can understand why a worker was picked.
- No significant perf regressions.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-37hc.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Ring buffer cap with eviction policy.
- Redact sensitive fields (env values, secrets).
- Concurrent selections must not corrupt log ordering.

## E2E Outline
- Run multiple selections -> audit log shows ordered entries.
- JSON endpoint/CLI output includes scores + reasons.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rchd/src/selection.rs: decision log record format + eviction policy.
- rchd/src/api.rs: expose decision log endpoint.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-37hc.sh: run multiple selections and verify ordered audit output.
